mechanism_narrative: The current high federal funds rate level above 5% imposes a restrictive monetary stance that raises the cost of capital for corporations and reduces present value of future earnings, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward cumulative return of the S&P 500 is positive in more than 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above the 4.0% threshold signals a weakening labor market that depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises from current level to exceed 4.0%
failure_condition: If after UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, the 60-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of instances, or if the signal's Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows stays below 0.03 absolute, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous monthly decline in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates a broad-based weakening in consumer and residential demand, forecasting short-term negative equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: Both RSAFS and HOUST record a month-over-month decline in their respective values
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of instances when both proxies decline simultaneously over a one-year evaluation period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime defined by CPIAUCSL above 300, an acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) signals overheating that prompts the Federal Reserve to maintain or tighten policy, negatively affecting equity valuations. In low inflation, rising production is positive.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL > 300 and INDPRO increases more than 0.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) in high inflation regimes when INDPRO surges, or if the sign flips direction in walk-forward testing, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A very low unemployment rate below 4% signals a tight labor market that supports robust wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns over the ensuing 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE is below 4.0%, or if the rank correlation between the UNRATE level and forward returns is positive (contradicting the hypothesis), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP (GDPC1) indicates a contraction in aggregate output and economic activity, historically preceding negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days as recession fears materialize.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines quarter-over-quarter from the previous published value
failure_condition: If after a GDPC1 decline, the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is positive in more than 40% of observations over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A joint month-over-month increase in both the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and the CPI (CPIAUCSL) confirms accelerating inflation and tightening monetary conditions, which should compress equity valuations over the very short term (5 trading days).
candidate_trigger: Both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL increase month-over-month
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of instances when both series rise, or if the average return after such joint increases is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the economy is near full employment (UNRATE below 4%), a decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) is a highly reliable negative signal of an upcoming recession, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. In high-unemployment regimes, this signal is weaker.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) when the trigger condition is met, or if the signal's predictive accuracy (directional hit rate) is below 55% over a 3-year walk-forward, the hypothesis is rejected.
